nativeRange.habitat: Coastal seafloors at depths of about 1–15 m, generally over sand and rubble adjacent to reefs.

decorPreferences: open swimming space along the bottom

diet.notes: Generally accepts a varied diet of meaty marine foods; mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, chopped seafood, and quality pellets/flakes are commonly recommended. Tends to do best with several small feedings per day.

avoidWith: large aggressive fish that may bully bottom-dwellers

summary: The Bluespotted Watchman Goby (Cryptocentrus pavoninoides) is a sand-dwelling Indo-Pacific shrimp goby that is generally peaceful toward other reef fish but strongly territorial with its own kind. It is best known for pairing with pistol shrimp, which dig and maintain the burrow both animals share.
careNotes: The Bluespotted Watchman Goby is generally considered a hardy, beginner-friendly species and one of the larger members of the Cryptocentrus genus, reaching around 12–15 cm as an adult. It is broadly regarded as peaceful toward unrelated tankmates but strongly territorial toward conspecifics and other shrimp gobies, so it is normally kept singly or as an established mated pair. It has been reported to form a classic symbiotic relationship with pistol shrimp (Alpheus spp.) — the shrimp digs and maintains a burrow that the goby uses as a refuge and lookout post, while the goby's sharper eyesight alerts the nearly blind shrimp to danger. A sandy substrate of at least a few centimetres plus loose coral rubble is generally advised so the pair can construct stable burrows; open bottom space is also recommended for foraging and territorial spacing. Given the species' adult size and active use of the bottom, a minimum of around 120 L with a tank footprint of roughly 90 cm is considered a more appropriate minimum than the smaller figures sometimes listed for this species. Like most shrimp gobies, the Bluespotted Watchman Goby is a known jumper, and a tightly-sealed lid is strongly recommended. It has also been reported to be aggressive toward very small ornamental shrimp, so housing it with delicate species such as sexy shrimp or anemone shrimp is generally discouraged.

primary.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
primary.notes: Primary source after LiveAquaria did not list this species; BlueZoo is the listed manifest secondary and was used as primary for sizing/care level/temperament.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Cryptocentrus pavoninoides)
additional.notes: Taxonomy (Gobiidae, Gobiiformes), maximum length 15 cm SL, depth range 1–15 m, native range Western Central Pacific, synonym Gobius pavoninoides Bleeker 1849.
